title: Living in Boulder 3 - 30 - 2017: 640 Kilometers in background

enrichment.summary: Kyle logs his day on March 30, 2017 late at night. He lost several hours of work when his computer crashed, wiping tracing work that involves 1600 different tracings. He also discusses his ongoing study of libertarianism, his response to their 'people are lazy' argument via a 'by schools' (bicycle?) daily routine concept, and his research into dark money by reading Jane Mayer's book 'Dark Money' while pausing to Wikipedia foundations and institutes she mentions.
enrichment.speaker: kyle

stances.position: Has a counter — a daily routine (biking/by schools) is self-fixing and makes laziness a non-issue
Studying it to understand how to redirect finances; implies opposition to dark money influence
Actively leaving comments on libertarian content and saving them for his own website

transcript.text: Hello, living in Boulder V-Log for March 30th, because right now it's March 31st, it's 2.31 in the morning.
So, today I got up at like 11 or something, because I was working until like 1 or 2.
And I was just tracing stuff all day long, like yesterday.
I got set back yesterday, and maybe it was more than two hours, maybe it was like four hours, you know, maybe it was in time warp or something.
But the computer just crashed in front of me, and it lost everything, so.
Or maybe it's just bigger than I thought.
It has 1,600 different tracings.
So, to make that on a grid on my site is going to be interesting, especially for the solo.
I'm looking forward to that.
I don't know how long it's going to take, though, but man, it's worth another week.
And I don't think I'll be able to do anything just on my car until I'm done with this.
My foot's getting dry and flaky.
Anyway, I'm still studying up on all of the Libertarians.
That's my project now.
I'm reading, I'm listening to Libertarian, and now I'm actually commenting.
Before I didn't comment, now I'm commenting and leaving my website on all my comments.
Because, you know, I have the economic alternative.
The Libertarians say that people are lazy, and so you shouldn't give them anything, because then I'll just be lazy.

But I have the bike school is the perfect fix to that, because in order to ride your bike all day, every day, and listen to an audiobook, and then do, like, two hours a day of talking, or at least an hour a day of talking, how could you do that and not be lazy?
You know what I mean?
It's a self-fixing system.
And then, of course, everybody want to do that, because it's so fun.
And then, of course, nobody's lazy.
So there you go.
Everybody deserves it.
And it's so cheap.
You know what I mean?
So there's no reason not to do it.
The answer.
But, yeah, you know, the Libertarian side, they delete everything.
So now I know to copy, because then I can then grab it later, because I'm writing a lot, grab it later, and then write stuff for my own thing on my website whenever I get around to it.
I can't type for shit on this computer, though.
I'm like, it's like typing something totally different.
If I want to type, I need to do it with the other one.
I want to experiment with the dictation of it.
And so, anyways, the backgrounds are 640, 640 kilometers in diameter, which is like, I don't know, tribe?
You see, I haven't made the website yet.
I haven't made the webpage yet, so I don't have it memorized.

Yeah, so I was listening to the book, Dark Money by Jane Mayer, and then whenever she mentions a foundation or an institute or a person, you know, I'll put that there book on pause, and now I'll Wikipedia it, and I'll watch it via YouTube of it.

To get myself caught up on the history of Dark Money so that I can divert it, because, you know, you have to, in order to direct the finances, you have to understand how it works in order to direct the finances, so.
I'll have plenty of ideas, I'm sure, later.
Hopefully it won't take too long.
Okay, so, anyways, four minutes, that's long enough.
Peace out.
